Package org.rodinp.core.indexer

Interface IPropagator

All Known Implementing Classes:
EventPropagator, IdentifierPropagator
public interface IPropagator
Common protocol for query propagation.

Implementors of this interface are intended to be used in calls to IIndexQuery.getOccurrences(IDeclaration, IPropagator), to propagate the call to declarations other than the given one. These other declarations are supposed to be extracted from the occurrences of the given one.

This can be particularly intended to deal with cases where various declarations represent the same entity. The problem is that each declaration is indexed separately, thus having separate occurrences. The propagation mechanism allows to get all occurrences of the entity, whatever the underlying declaration. It relies on the hypothesis that there is a way to link occurrences of a declaration to the other target declarations.

The way declarations are linked to each other through occurrences depends on indexers implementation. Such a link may or may not exist.

This interface is intended to be implemented by clients.

Since:
1.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    IDeclaration
    getRelativeDeclaration(IOccurrence occurrence, IIndexQuery query)
    Returns the declaration to propagate the query on, from the given occurrence.

  • Method Details

    • getRelativeDeclaration

      IDeclaration getRelativeDeclaration(IOccurrence occurrence, IIndexQuery query)
      Returns the declaration to propagate the query on, from the given occurrence. The propagator decides whether to propagate or not, and what to propagate on.
      Parameters:
      occurrence - an occurrence found while performing a propagating query
      query - the calling query
      Returns:
      the declaration of the element to propagate the query on, or null if the occurrence involves no propagation
      See Also:
      • IIndexQuery.getOccurrences(IDeclaration, IPropagator)
